title: clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. mashariki
intro: Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. mashariki is a plant species native to East Africa, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. mashariki include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
